description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block pillar with a slightly tapered body and a flat, overhanging capstone at the top. The main body is constructed from interlocking, rectangular stone segments in various shades of brown, giving it a masonry appearance. The base and cap are made of a gray stone material, both wider than the central shaft, providing stability and a finished look. The surface of the stones is matte and shows subtle, hand-painted texturing, with visible mortar lines between the blocks. There are no visible engravings or decorative elements, and the block appears solid and robust. The overall design suggests it is intended as a functional urban barrier or boundary marker.
description: A rectangular stone pillar with a gray cap and base, featuring interlocking brown masonry blocks and a sturdy, matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view shows the stone pillar's interlocking block pattern with a prominent vertical seam and a slightly wider gray cap and base.
description_view_1: Side view reveals a different arrangement of the stone blocks, with fewer vertical seams and a similar cap and base structure.
description_view_2: Opposite side view closely resembles view 1, with a nearly identical block pattern and the same cap and base proportions.
description_view_3: Back view is almost identical to views 1 and 2, showing the same block arrangement and structural features.
attribute_weathering_pattern: Slight discoloration and surface wear on the cap and base, with minor surface irregularities suggesting exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or used as a standalone boundary marker
